From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Central business district of Rotorua, New Zealand

Main article: [Rotorua](/wiki/Rotorua "Rotorua")

Suburb in Rotorua, New Zealand

**Rotorua Central** is the central business district and central suburb of Rotorua, in the [Bay of Plenty](/wiki/Bay_of_Plenty "Bay of Plenty") region of New Zealand's [North Island](/wiki/North_Island "North Island").

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Central&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Demographics")]

Rotorua Central covers 1.77 km2 (0.68 sq mi)[[1]](#cite_note-Area-1) and had an estimated population of 540 as of June 2025,[[2]](#cite_note-NZ_population_data_2023_SA2-2) with a population density of 305 people per km2.

Historical population

| Year | Pop. | ±% p.a. |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2006 | 471 | — |
| 2013 | 528 | +1.65% |
| 2018 | 537 | +0.34% |
| 2023 | 513 | −0.91% |
| Source: [[3]](#cite_note-Census_2023-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Census_2018-4) | | |

Rotorua Central had a population of 513 in the [2023 New Zealand census](/wiki/2023_New_Zealand_census "2023 New Zealand census"), a decrease of 24 people (−4.5%) since the [2018 census](/wiki/2018_New_Zealand_census "2018 New Zealand census"), and a decrease of 15 people (−2.8%) since the [2013 census](/wiki/2013_New_Zealand_census "2013 New Zealand census"). There were 279 males, 231 females, and 3 people of [other genders](/wiki/Non-binary_gender "Non-binary gender") in 240 dwellings.[[5]](#cite_note-5) 4.7% of people identified as [LGBTIQ+](/wiki/LGBTQ "LGBTQ"). The median age was 41.2 years (compared with 38.1 years nationally). There were 33 people (6.4%) aged under 15 years, 114 (22.2%) aged 15 to 29, 297 (57.9%) aged 30 to 64, and 69 (13.5%) aged 65 or older.[[3]](#cite_note-Census_2023-3)

People could identify as more than one ethnicity. The results were 46.2% [European](/wiki/European_New_Zealanders "European New Zealanders") ([Pākehā](/wiki/P%C4%81keh%C4%81 "Pākehā")); 40.9% [Māori](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_people "Māori people"); 3.5% [Pasifika](/wiki/Pasifika_New_Zealanders "Pasifika New Zealanders"); 23.4% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_New_Zealanders "Asian New Zealanders"); 1.8% Middle Eastern, Latin American and African New Zealanders (MELAA); and 1.2% other, which includes people giving their ethnicity as "New Zealander". English was spoken by 93.0%, Māori by 15.2%, Samoan by 0.6%, and other languages by 20.5%. No language could be spoken by 1.8% (e.g. too young to talk). [New Zealand Sign Language](/wiki/New_Zealand_Sign_Language "New Zealand Sign Language") was known by 1.2%. The percentage of people born overseas was 33.3, compared with 28.8% nationally.[[3]](#cite_note-Census_2023-3)

Religious affiliations were 29.8% [Christian](/wiki/Christianity_in_New_Zealand "Christianity in New Zealand"), 5.8% [Hindu](/wiki/Hinduism_in_New_Zealand "Hinduism in New Zealand"), 1.8% [Islam](/wiki/Islam_in_New_Zealand "Islam in New Zealand"), 4.1% [Māori religious beliefs](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_religious_beliefs "Māori religious beliefs"), 2.9% [Buddhist](/wiki/Buddhism_in_New_Zealand "Buddhism in New Zealand"), and 1.8% other religions. People who answered that they had [no religion](/wiki/Irreligion_in_New_Zealand "Irreligion in New Zealand") were 47.4%, and 6.4% of people did not answer the census question.[[3]](#cite_note-Census_2023-3)

Of those at least 15 years old, 96 (20.0%) people had a bachelor's or higher degree, 219 (45.6%) had a post-high school certificate or diploma, and 162 (33.8%) people exclusively held high school qualifications. The median income was $33,600, compared with $41,500 nationally. 36 people (7.5%) earned over $100,000 compared to 12.1% nationally. The employment status of those at least 15 was 228 (47.5%) full-time, 60 (12.5%) part-time, and 51 (10.6%) unemployed.[[3]](#cite_note-Census_2023-3)

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Central&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Economy")]

### Retail

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Central&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Retail")]

The Rotorua Central mall opened in August 1995.[[6]](#cite_note-RotoruaCentralAbout-6) It has 55 tenants, including [The Warehouse](/wiki/The_Warehouse_Group "The Warehouse Group"), [Harvey Norman](/wiki/Harvey_Norman "Harvey Norman"), [Countdown](/wiki/Countdown_(supermarket) "Countdown (supermarket)"), [Farmers](/wiki/Farmers_(department_store) "Farmers (department store)"), [Briscoes](/wiki/Briscoes "Briscoes"), [Rebel Sport](/wiki/Rebel_Sport_(New_Zealand) "Rebel Sport (New Zealand)") and [Smiths City](/wiki/Smiths_City "Smiths City").[[7]](#cite_note-RotoruaCentralDirectory-7)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Central&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Education")]

Rotorua Primary School is a co-educational Year 1 to 8 school,[[8]](#cite_note-official1934-8)[[9]](#cite_note-moe1934-9) with a roll of 406 (as of October 2025).[[10]](#cite_note-NZ_school_roll_data-10)[[11]](#cite_note-ero1934-11) It started in 1886 as Rotorua Public School, and students from a [native school](/wiki/Native_schools "Native schools") established in 1868 joined them over the next couple of years. In 1887 it moved to the current site.[[12]](#cite_note-12) It added a Secondary Department in 1914 to become Rotorua District High School. The Secondary Department moved to a succession of new sites in the 1920s.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
